question: The pie chart shows the distribution of courses offered by students. What percentage of the students offer English?
Find the value of x at the minimum point of the curve y = x<sup>3</sup> + x<sup>2</sup> - x + 1
solution:
y = x<sup>3</sup> + x<sup>2</sup> - x + 1

dy/dx = d(x<sup>3</sup>)/dx + d(x<sup>2</sup>)/dx - d(x)/dx + d(1)/dx


dy/dx = 3x<sup>2</sup> + 2x - 1 = 0

dy/dx = 3x<sup>2</sup> + 2x - 1

At the maximum point dy/dx
 = 0

3x<sup>2</sup> + 2x - 1 = 0

(3x<sup>2</sup> + 3x) - (x - 1) = 0

3x(x + 1) -1(x + 1) = 0

(3x - 1)(x + 1) = 0

therefore x = 1/3 or -1

For the maximum point

d<sup>2</sup>y/dx<sup>2</sup> < 0

d<sup>2</sup>y/dx<sup>2</sup> 6x + 2

when x = 1/3


dx<sup>2</sup>/dx<sup>2</sup>
 = 6(1/3) + 2

= 2 + 2 = 4

d<sup>2</sup>y/dx<sup>2</sup>, > o which is the minimum point

when x = -1

d<sup>2</sup>y/dx<sup>2</sup> = 6(-1) + 2

= -6 + 2 = -4

-4 < 0

therefore, d<sup>2</sup>y/dx<sup>2</sup> < 0

the minimum point is 1/3

question: If log<sub>3</sub>18 + log<sub>3</sub>3 - log<sub>3</sub>x = 3, Find x.
solution:
log<sub>3</sub>18
 + log<sub>3</sub>3
 - log<sub>3</sub>x
 = 3

log<sub>3</sub>18
 + log<sub>3</sub>3
 - log<sub>3</sub>x
 = 3log<sub>3</sub>3

log<sub>3</sub>18
 + log<sub>3</sub>3
 - log<sub>3</sub>x
 = log<sub>3</sub>3<sup>3</sup>

log<sub>3</sub>(18×3/X
) = log<sub>3</sub>3<sup>3</sup>

18×3/X
 = 3<sup>3</sup>

18 x 3 = 27 x X

x = 18×3/27


= 2

solution:
Mode = L<sub>1</sub> + (D<sub>1</sub/D<sub>1</sub>+D<sub>2</sub>)C

D<sub>1</sub> = frequency of modal class - frequency of the class before it

D<sub>1</sub> = 5 - 2 = 3

D<sub>2</sub> = frequency of modal class - frequency of the class that offers it

D<sub>2</sub> = 5 - 3 = 2

L<sub>1</sub> = lower class boundary of the modal class

L<sub>1</sub> = 5 - 5

C is the class width = 8 - 5.5 = 3

Mode = L<sub>1</sub> + (D<sub>1</sub>/D<sub>1</sub>+D<sub>2</sub>)C

= 5.5 + 3/(2+3)C

= 5.5 + 3/5 x 3

= 5.5 + 9/5

= 5.5 + 1.8

= 7.3 ≈ = 7

question: A man invested N5,000 for 9 months at 4%. What is the simple interest?
solution:
S.I. = P×R×T/100


If T = 9 months, it is equivalent to 9/12
 years

S.I. = 5000×4×9/(100×12)


S.I. = N150
